column1: # Layout matches arduino-esp32's "min_spiffs" partition scheme so an OTA
# from Arduino-firmware → IDF-firmware writes to the same slot the running
# fielded ESP32-CAM units running the .ino can't OTA to the IDF build —
# the dashboard's BLE OTA only updates the app bin
# table. Each app slot is 1920K — the .ino has fit comfortably; the IDF
# build is currently ~1.5MB with WebRTC + camera + BLE all in.
# spiffs / coredump partitions from arduino-esp32's layout are dropped
# here — the IDF build doesn't use SPIFFS (NVS handles all persistence)
# and we'd rather have the flash for headroom.
